tickets.title: US001: Menu Filter
tickets.description:
As a student, I want to be able to filter ingredients as checkboxes, so that I can avoid stuff that I can't consume.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Filtered food mustn't appear in the filtered menu.
2. Loading menu can only take less than 5 seconds.
3. Food user interface mustn't be outside of the menu box user interface.

tickets.title: US002: Menu sorting
tickets.description:
As a student, I want the app to be able to sort food by price, sales, ratings ascending or descending, so that I can save time ordering food.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Sorting feature must only include filtered products (US001)
2. Sorting must take less than 3 seconds
3. All products must show correctly in ascending or descending order.

tickets.title: US003: Online ordering
tickets.description:
As a student, I want to be able to place order online with multiple products, so that I can save some time waiting.

Acceptance criteria:
1. The order must show all the ordered products.
2. The student can order at any distance.
3. The order must be sent to the main cafeteria in < 2 seconds.

tickets.title: US004: Sold out product
tickets.description:
As a Chef, I want to be able to mark an item as Sold Out and cancel all pending orders with the item, so that students won't order ran out food.

Acceptance criteria:
1. The process (marking item and cancel orders) must take less than 5 seconds.
2. All pending orders about ran out items must be cancelled
3. The system should be able to handle at least 3000 orders.

analysis.ambiguity1: "I need to filter the entire menu" is unclear. We don't know how the filter should work (whitelisting / blacklisting, sorting food descending or ascending by price, include only drinks / foods,...).
analysis.ambiguity2: "I need to be able to mark an item as Sold Out instantly" is vague. We don't know how fast the feature should be (200 miliseconds or 5 seconds or 10 minutes,...).
analysis.conflictSolution: Conflict between Cafeteria Manager: "I want students to order food on their phone while they are walking from class" and Chef: "I need to be able to mark an item as Sold Out instantly" because the students can still order ran out items before they mark them as Sold Out instantly. The solution is to delete all orders of the item when they are marked as Sold Out instantly, and send notifications to the students who ordered the ran out items.
analysis.nfr1: Reliability: Filtered menu must show no filtered items
analysis.nfr2: Reliability: system must still work offline for at least 10 minutes without crashing if the internet cuts out
analysis.nfr3: Performance: Push notfications must be sent to student at 11:30 AM
